member.name: Andrew Griffith
member.role: MP for Arundel and South Downs
member.synopsis: Andrew Griffith is the Conservative MP for Arundel and South Downs , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Business and Trade.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when the Minister of State for Rail plans to respond to the letters of (a) 23 April and (b) 3 June from the hon. Member for Arundel and South Downs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A response was sent on 01 July 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Redundancy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, how many advance notifications of redundancy were received by the Insolvency Service's Redundancy Payments Service in each financial year from 2019-20 to 2025-26 by (a) 20 to 50 proposed redundancies,
writtenQuestions.answerText: Please see ‘advanced notifications of redundancy’ received by the Redundancy Payments Service for the requested Period.Financial Year / Advance Notice of Redundancy20-50 51-100 101-250 250+ 2019-20202,1747774201532020-20216,9892,1278663582021-20221,74...

writtenQuestions.heading: Project Gigabit: Arundel and South Downs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how many premises in Arundel and South Downs constituency were included in the original Project Gigabit contract awarded to CityFibre for East and West Sussex; and how many premises are
writtenQuestions.answerText: Approximately 6,490 premises in the Arundel and South Downs constituency were included in the initial scope of the Project Gigabit contract for East and West Sussex, which is being delivered by CityFibre.Building Digital UK (BDUK) and CityFibre have since...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Agreements: United Arab Emirates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to negotiate a free trade agreement between the United Kingdom and the UAE.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK concluded a Free Trade Agreement with the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on 20 May, which will strengthen trade and investment ties with all six member states.The UK’s focus is working with the UAE and GCC towards signature and entry into force of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Agreements: Mercosur
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to negotiate a free trade agreement between the United Kingdom and Mercosur.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Mercosur countries are important partners for the UK. We are committed to strengthening our bilateral trading relationships to remove barriers to trade and help grow UK exports, which is why I visited Brazil soon after I took up post in 2025, where I sign...

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, when he plans to respond to the letters of 10 April and 12 May 2026 from the hon. Member for Arundel and South Downs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Business and Trade aims to respond to correspondence in 15 working days, but in some instances it can take longer. The Department has conducted a search of its records and has not identified the correspondence referred to, and will respo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, when she intends to respond to the letter of 14 April 2026 from the hon. Member for Arundel and South Downs on a constituent's complaint about the Financial Ombudsman Service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The letter of 14 April from the hon. Member for Arundel and South Downs is currently receiving urgent attention under reference MC2026/08512. HM Treasury will respond in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: Magnetic Resonance Imagers: Helium
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans he has to ensure reliable and affordable supplies of Helium for MRI scanners in the NHS estate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is closely monitoring the impacts of the Middle East conflict on the medical supply chain, including on the supply of helium. We have engaged with industry and received assurance on the stability of continued helium supply through contingen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Helium: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what plans she has to ensure reliable and affordable supplies of Helium in the use of research funded by her department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of reliable and affordable helium supplies for UK research. We are strengthening resilience across critical supply chains including through diversifying supply sources and supporting domestic capability, where appr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Manufacturing Industries: Helium
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what consideration he has given to securing reliable supplies of Helium for use in advanced manufacturing, aerospace and defence in the United Kingdom and Northern Ireland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A secure supply of critical minerals, like helium, is vital for the UK’s economic growth and security, industrial strategy, and clean energy transition Through the 2025 UK Critical Minerals Strategy we are building will build strong partnerships with key ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Authority Housing Fund: West Sussex
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many properties have been purchased by the Local Authority Housing Fund in (a) the Chichester District, (b) the Horsham District, (c) the Arun District and (d) aggregate for
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 2022, there have been several rounds of properties purchased through the Local Authority Housing Fund. Round 1 (delivered between 2022- 24), Round 2 (delivered between 2023-25) and Round 3 (delivered between 2024-26). Chichester has delivered 18 hom...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Business and Trade: Consultants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, with reference to the Department for Business and Trade workforce management information return for March 2026, for what reason the Department recorded consultancy spend of -£20,783,968.13; whether thi
writtenQuestions.answerText: In March 2026 an adjustment was made to correctly reclassify £14.9m of 2025-26 costs from consultancy to professional services. In addition, there was a correction of a purchase order receipt which had incorrectly recorded £9.5m of consultancy costs in Fe...

writtenQuestions.heading: British Students Abroad: USA
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many UK-domiciled students received financial support from Government-backed mobility schemes to undertake a period of study at universities in the United States in 2021–22, 2022–23 and 2023–24.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department holds data on students at UK higher education (HE) providers who receive Turing Scheme funding for overseas placements. The department does not hold data on student domicile and figures therefore include international students. Students can...

items.text: Role, work or services: Non-recurring podcast guest Until: 17 March 2026. Payer: The Rigby Foundation (The Rigby Foundation is a place based funder with the majority of donations committed to projects supporting children and young people aged 11 years to 25 years from disadvantaged backgrounds.), Rigby Group (RG) PLC Bridgeway House, Bridgeway, Stratford-Upon-Avon CV37 6YX (Registered 30 March 2026)

Payment: £1,000 The Rigby Foundation directly donated £1,000 to The Sussex Snowdrop Trust, a charity in my constituency providing 'Nursing Care at Home’ for local children who have a life-threatening or terminal illness, following a podcast I joined on one occasion. Received on: 27 March 2026. Hours: 1 hr. Paid directly to: a charity (Registered 31 March 2026)
